summary: Stocks snapped a five-week losing streak on optimism that the Iran war could end sooner than feared, while a blowout March jobs report — 178,000 payrolls vs. a 59,000 consensus — landed with markets closed for Good Friday and remains unpriced. That jobs beat complicates the Fed's path as oil-driven inflation persists, keeping sentiment fragile across all asset classes heading into Monday's open.
tweet: Iran war ceasefire hopes drove a relief rally, but a scorching March jobs print keeps rate-cut hopes near zero — markets reprice everything Monday.
